Function Reference

Complete API reference for taxodist in R and Python. All functions are structurally equivalent across both ecosystems.

Distance Computation

taxo_distance()

Signature: taxo_distance(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Computes the taxonomic distance between two taxa. Retrieves both lineages from The Taxonomicon, finds their most recent common ancestor (MRCA), and calculates the distance as \(1 / \text{depth}(\text{MRCA})\).

  • Returns: named list (R) or dict (Python) containing the following fields:
    • distance: Numeric taxonomic distance.
    • mrca: Name of the most recent common ancestor.
    • mrca_depth: Hierarchical node depth of the MRCA.
    • depth_a / depth_b: Absolute lineage depths of both input taxa.

distance_matrix()

Signature: distance_matrix(taxa)

Computes the full symmetric pairwise distance matrix for a vector of taxon names. Lineages are cached in memory after first retrieval to avoid redundant network requests.

  • Returns: Standard dist object in R, or a scipy-compatible distance matrix (NumPy ndarray) in Python, with zeros on the diagonal. Taxa not present in the database are populated with NA / None distances.

focal_distances()

Signature: focal_distances(focal, community)

Computes taxonomic distances from a single focal taxon to all members of a community vector. This is equivalent to pulling a single row of a complete distance matrix, but operates with higher execution efficiency.

  • Returns: named numeric vector (R) or pandas Series (Python).

closest_relative()

Signature: closest_relative(query, candidates)

Identifies and ranks candidate taxa based on their taxonomic proximity to a query taxon. Useful for nearest-neighbor classification or phylogenetic sorting.

  • Returns: data.frame (R) or pandas DataFrame (Python) containing columns taxon and distance (sorted in ascending order).

mrca()

Signature: mrca(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Retrieves the name of the most recent common ancestor of two taxa as recorded in The Taxonomicon classification hierarchy.

  • Returns: character string (R) or str (Python).

Lineage Utilities

get_lineage()

Signature: get_lineage(taxon)

Retrieves the full hierarchical lineage of a taxon by name, from the root node (Biota) down to the taxon itself. This function resolves classifications at any rank, including species, genus, family, order, or named clades. All successfully resolved lineages are stored in the local session cache.

  • Returns: character vector (R) or list of strings (Python).

get_lineage_by_id()

Signature: get_lineage_by_id(id)

Retrieves and parses a complete hierarchical lineage using The Taxonomicon’s unique numeric identifier instead of a text name. This method bypasses name resolution and is recommended for programmatically resolving homonyms or historical classifications.

  • Returns: character vector (R) or list of strings (Python).

get_taxonomicon_id()

Signature: get_taxonomicon_id(taxon)

Resolves a text taxon name to its corresponding numeric Taxonomicon database identifier. It returns the identifier of the first valid taxonomic match. For highly ambiguous queries or homonyms, use taxo_search() first to inspect all possible matches.

  • Returns: integer (R) or int (Python).

taxo_search()

Signature: taxo_search(query)

Queries The Taxonomicon database for all biological entries matching the specified query string. This is used to resolve homonym ambiguities, evaluate taxonomic synonyms, or locate the precise numeric database ID for a genus or species.

  • Returns: data.frame (R) or pandas DataFrame (Python) containing columns:
    • id: The numeric Taxonomicon ID.
    • name: The full taxon description, including author and publication year annotations.
    • rank: The assigned taxonomic rank.
    • accepted_name: The current accepted name according to Systema Naturae 2000.

compare_lineages()

Signature: compare_lineages(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Generates a comparative side-by-side printout of two complete taxonomic lineages. It aligns both hierarchies, isolating the shared ancestral trunk up to their most recent common ancestor, and lists the unique diverging clades for each taxon below the split.

  • Returns: Invisibly returns a list (R) or dict (Python) containing the following fields:
    • shared: Lineage path shared by both taxa up to the MRCA.
    • unique_a: Unique ancestral nodes for taxon A.
    • unique_b: Unique ancestral nodes for taxon B.

shared_clades()

Signature: shared_clades(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Extracts the shared portion of the taxonomic lineage between two taxa, representing all overlapping hierarchical nodes from the root (Biota) down to and including their MRCA.

  • Returns: character vector (R) or list of strings (Python).

taxo_path()

Signature: taxo_path(taxon_a, taxon_b)

Traces the node-by-node path connecting two taxa through the taxonomic hierarchy, ascending from taxon A up to their MRCA, and then descending down to taxon B. This maps the shortest topological path between both entities.

  • Returns: character vector (R) or list of strings (Python).

lineage_depth()

Signature: lineage_depth(taxon)

Calculates the absolute number of taxonomic nodes present in a taxon’s lineage from root to tip. This measures how fully resolved the hierarchy of a given clade is within The Taxonomicon database.

  • Returns: integer (R) or int (Python).

is_member()

Signature: is_member(taxon, clade)

Evaluates whether a target taxon is nested within a specified clade. This is determined by verifying whether the target clade name is present in the taxon’s hierarchical lineage.

  • Returns: logical (R) or bool (Python).

filter_clade()

Signature: filter_clade(taxa, clade)

Filters a vector of taxonomic names, retaining only those individuals that belong to the specified clade. This serves as a set operation tool to isolate specific evolutionary groups from broad ecological communities.

  • Returns: character vector (R) or list of strings (Python).

check_coverage()

Signature: check_coverage(taxa)

Cross-references a list of taxon names against The Taxonomicon database to verify their presence. This serves as a pre-screening step before executing larger distance computations, as unrecognized taxa will return NA distance values.

  • Returns: Named logical vector (R) or pandas Series of booleans (Python).

Multivariate Analysis

taxo_cluster()

Signature: taxo_cluster(taxa, method = "average")

Executes hierarchical clustering on a calculated taxonomic distance matrix. The default clustering algorithm is UPGMA ("average"), which preserves taxonomic distance properties. The output object integrates directly with standard plotting methods and phylogenetics pipelines.

  • Returns: A custom list object containing:
    • hclust: The standard R hclust object (which can be cast to a phylogenetic tree via ape::as.phylo()).
    • dist: The underlying symmetric taxonomic distance matrix.

taxo_ordinate()

Signature: taxo_ordinate(taxa_or_dist)

Applies classical multidimensional scaling (Principal Coordinate Analysis, PCoA) to project a taxonomic distance matrix into a low-dimensional Euclidean space. This function accepts either a raw vector of taxon names or a pre-computed distance matrix of class "dist".

  • Returns: A structured list containing:
    • points: A numeric coordinate matrix of dimensions \(N \times K\).
    • eigenvalues: Eigenvalues computed for each PCoA axis.
    • gof: Goodness-of-fit statistics.

taxo_heatmap()

Signature: taxo_heatmap(taxa_or_dist)

Generates a spatial distance heatmap with marginal dendrograms. The rows and columns are automatically reordered based on UPGMA hierarchical clustering. It accepts either a raw list of taxa or a pre-computed distance matrix.

  • Returns: Invisibly returns the underlying plot object or active graphical device.

Cache Management

clear_cache()

Signature: clear_cache()

Purges all taxonomic lineages and database identifiers currently preserved in the session-level memory cache. Subsequent calculations will require fresh HTTP queries to the database server.

  • Returns: Invisibly returns NULL (R) or None (Python).

save_cache()

Signature: save_cache(path)

Serializes the active in-memory session cache to disk as an .rds file (R) or a .pkl file (Python). This preserves parsed classifications and avoids repeating network requests in future analytical sessions.

  • Returns: Invisibly returns the serialized path string.

load_cache()

Signature: load_cache(path)

Restores a serialized cache from disk back into your active programming environment. This merges the stored classifications with your current session cache, preventing redundant network requests.

  • Returns: Invisibly returns NULL (R) or None (Python).

cache_info()

Signature: cache_info()

Generates a diagnostic report summarizing the active session cache. It reports the total number of cached lineages, resolved database identifiers, active memory consumption (in bytes), and a preview of cached taxonomic names.

  • Returns: Prints structural summary statistics to the console, and invisibly returns a diagnostic list (R) or dict (Python).
